title: sophora microphylla subsp. macnabiana
intro: Sophora microphylla subsp. macnabiana, also known as Kowhai, is a subalpine shrub native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Kowhai include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Kowhai is traditionally prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations.
